content: sd plan submit takes 0-based step indices in the 'blocks' arrays (e.g. step 0 with blocks:[1] means step 0 blocks step 1); 'sd plan show' renders them 1-based for humans. Validation error 'step index N is out of range (have N steps)' means the index equals or exceeds the step count — convert 1-based draft to 0-based before submit. Verified against pl-2f15 (warren R-06 cron scheduler plan, 8 steps).

title: pi as third built-in runtime: parity-first phased plan (pl-4374)
rationale: Picked phased landing (parity → customization → multi-provider → cost → event-model → docs) over a single mega-PR. Cross-repo burrow piRuntime is step 0 blocker for all warren-side work. Extensions deferred until canopy ships an artifact type (TS source in canopy sections is awkward). RPC mode chosen over JSON mode for bidirectional steer/abort/get_session_stats surface. Cost columns (cost_usd + tokens_*) trust pi's get_session_stats verbatim; warren-side price tables rejected. supportsResume=true (pi has --continue/--session); not codex-shaped.

name: pi runtime external research (pi-mono / @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent)
description: Pi is a TS coding-agent toolkit (badlogic/pi-mono, earendil-works/pi). CLI: pi (-p print, --mode json|rpc, --provider, --model, --thinking, -c/--continue, --session <path|id>, --skill, --prompt-template, -e/--extension). Multi-provider: Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/Bedrock/Vertex/DeepSeek/Groq/Mistral/Azure + custom via ~/.pi/agent/models.json. RPC commands include prompt/steer/abort/get_state/get_session_stats/get_messages/set_model/cycle_model/new_session/switch_session/fork/clone/bash/compact. Event kinds: agent_start/end, turn_start/end, message_start/update/end, tool_execution_start/update/end, queue_update, compaction_start/end, auto_retry_start/end, extension_error. get_session_stats returns {tokens:{input,output,cacheRead,cacheWrite,total}, cost (USD), contextUsage:{tokens,contextWindow,percent}}. Customization: .pi/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (agentskills.io standard), .pi/prompts/<name>.md (template vars {{var}}), .pi/extensions/*.ts (registerTool, registerCommand via ExtensionAPI). Sessions persist at ~/.pi/agent/sessions/. Docs: pi.dev/docs/latest/usage and github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/blob/main/packages/coding-agent/docs/rpc.md.

title: R-19 preview plan starts with SPEC §11.L design lock + burrow inbound-networking coord seed
rationale: R-19 is filed [proposed] / needs-sketching in ROADMAP: the sketch touches burrow networking policy, warren proxy + routing, TLS / wildcard certs, signed-cookie auth, and TTL+LRU lifecycle. pl-2c59 makes step 1 (SPEC §11.L + a burrow-side seed for inbound networking) a gate on every other step so the design lands before code, mirroring how pl-5d74 (R-02) and pl-49f3 (V1 phased plan) deferred consumption to follow-up seeds when the schema seam wasn't yet stable. Same-sandbox default + signed-cookie auth + port-allocator-in-runs-table are picked here per ROADMAP open-question prose; R-12 cross-host routing is explicitly deferred. Reap-time launch is the 5th best-effort sub-step, reusing the pr_open pattern (mx-05abb2) so a preview failure never fails the run.

title: Frame R-13 as async-repo refactor first, then add Postgres backend
rationale: bun-sqlite (sync) → node-postgres (async) is the bigger blast radius (~51 sync call sites across 7 repos + ~30 test files), not the dialect mapping. Plan pl-f17e lands step 1 as a pure-refactor PR with SQLite still as the only backend so any regression is bisectable against mechanical changes. ROADMAP R-13's 'Drizzle Kit emit both dialects from one schema file' sketch is approximate — drizzle-orm has no shared sqlite/pg table builder, so the plan calls for parallel src/db/schema/{sqlite,postgres}.ts files backed by a shared src/db/schema/columns.ts constants module + a CI lint that diffs the two shapes. WARREN_DB_PATH stays as a back-compat alias that synthesizes a sqlite:// URL; WARREN_DB_URL is the new primary contract.

name: cross-repo-design-lock-seed
description: Phase 1 of any cross-repo plan freezes the design in SPEC.md §11.* AND files the dependency seed in the consumer repo's .seeds/ before any code lands. The warren-side SPEC names the upstream seed id (e.g. burrow-8647) inline so future readers navigate the seam without grepping; ROADMAP entry repeats the id at the bullet that depends on it. Used by warren-94d8 (R-19, plan pl-2c59): SPEC §11.L design lock + burrow-8647 filed before warren steps 3-6 could move.

title: V2 preview teardown reuses teardownPreview, does not extend EvictionReason
rationale: warren-38f7's seed framing was internally contradictory: it called for 'reuse the manual-teardown route' AND 'emit preview_evicted with reason=pr_closed'. The lock resolves toward teardownPreview because (a) the route's CAS already serializes against the eviction worker and manual route, so a webhook racing an idle-TTL sweep deterministically lands in exactly one; (b) preview_torn_down already carries a free-form actor field for attribution, so a structured tag ('github-webhook:pr_merged' / 'github-webhook:pr_closed_unmerged') distinguishes trigger source without growing the enum or adding a new event kind; (c) PR-close is an external trigger, not a periodic sweep, so it's semantically closer to manual teardown than to worker eviction. The seed framing predated the warren-d725 split between preview_torn_down (operator/external, carries actor) and preview_evicted (worker sweep, carries EvictionReason). Locking the design now (rather than waiting for webhook infra) keeps the eviction story in one place and prevents re-derivation when the receiver lands. SPEC §11.N is the canonical home; implementation seed warren-38f7 stays open as the tracker.

content: When a plan step's title or description includes a '(warren-XXXX fold-in)' marker (e.g. pl-f1be step warren-670f folding in warren-5549), close the folded-in seed in the same commit that closes the step, not just the step itself. Otherwise the folded seed lingers open until someone re-discovers it via 'sd ready'. The marker means 'this step subsumes the work of seed XXXX' — closing only the step leaves the parent seed dangling and forces a future agent to verify the work was actually done before closing.

When a ROADMAP R-XX ships, update its status line in-place with 'Status: [shipped] — landed via plan pl-XXXX (parent seed warren-XXXX), YYYY-MM-DD.' then a concrete summary of what shipped (migration filenames, new routes, schema PK changes, frontmatter labels, UI surfaces, acceptance scenario number). Convert the 'Open questions.' subsection to 'Follow-ups (filed as seeds; not in V1).' once those questions are filed as seeds in the plan's docs step. Examples: R-02 (pl-5d74), R-06 (pl-2f15), R-03 (pl-fef5).

name: pl-fef5 R-03 per-project canopy tier shipped clean
description: R-03 (warren-2842) shipped via 11-step plan pl-fef5 over 2026-05-15 with bun test (1361 pass / 15 skip / 0 fail), biome check --error-on-warnings, and tsc --noEmit all green on the final wrap-up step (warren-b21a) with no follow-up fixes. Schema migration (step 1, warren-094a) dropped agents.name PK + runs.agent_name FK for composite identity (project_id, name) plus partial unique on (name) WHERE project_id IS NULL, applied cleanly on both sqlite and postgres. Two follow-up seeds filed in step 10 (warren-b3d8): cross-tier inheritance (project extends library/builtin) and on-disk rendered cache under <projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/, both intentionally deferred from V2 scope. Reference point for future schema-touching plans: step-by-step decomposition with green gates per merge avoided integration debt.

title: Adopt Plot as warren's coordination primitive
rationale: The blog post 'Agentic Networks' (jayminwest.com/blog/12-agentic-networks) argues for a network topology with humans as peer nodes sharing a substrate, not orchestrators above workers. Plot is that substrate. Warren's current run-centric model reifies the hierarchical pattern; shifting to Plot-centric makes the network topology buildable. Plot ships as a standalone os-eco repo (not incubated inside warren) to avoid extraction switching costs (mulch re-domaining, comment rewrites, API reshaping) and to enable agent-side CLI use directly from sandboxes.

name: Agentic Networks blog post
description: https://www.jayminwest.com/blog/12-agentic-networks — argues against hierarchical orchestrator-to-sub-agent patterns in favor of a network topology where the human is a heavily weighted node sharing a context substrate with peer agents. Key claims: (1) traditional orchestrator-to-sub-agent delegation creates lossy translation layers between intent and execution; (2) winning systems will keep the human close while continuing to self-improve; (3) humans can't manage notifications from 50+ agents, so the system must rely on upfront human-authored intent that guides autonomous coordination. Foundational for warren's post-V1 UI direction and for the Plot primitive (../plot/SPEC.md). Cite this when justifying departures from run-centric / orchestrator-hierarchy patterns.

content: When a SPEC §11 phase-1 dispatch-wiring subsection lands for a multi-phase plan (warren-000b/pl-2047 Plot integration is the exemplar), the docs subsection should pin five things together: (1) the gating contract (both project flag AND per-run id must be present), (2) the env contract injected at the burrow.up call, (3) the ACL surface narrowed at the facade level (reference the constant by name, e.g. HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES), (4) an explicit ASCII data-flow diagram (spawn → reap), (5) what is deferred to later phases. Stating 'deferred to later phases' inline keeps follow-up plan readers from re-deriving the boundary. Mirror §11.N style — fire-and-log posture documented, idempotency story stated explicitly.

title: PlanRun coordinator state machine: 7-shape AdvanceResult union
rationale: src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts advancePlanRun returns a discriminated union {kind:'dispatched'|'waiting_for_run'|'waiting_for_merge'|'advanced'|'plan_failed'|'plan_succeeded'|'noop'}. The 'advanced' shape (merged previous child + dispatched the next on the same tick) is what makes plan progression feel interactive — without it the operator waits one full tick (10s default) between merge and dispatch. The 'noop' shape is the escape hatch for unexpected state (in-flight child missing runId, run row vanished, showSeed throws, succeeded run with no PR and no empty-push event but somehow still has prUrl null) — surface as a diagnostic without failing the plan or crashing the tick. Per-PlanRun errors inside advancePlanRun are caught at the tick layer (mx-fb4a85 risk #9 carried over). Resume semantics (warren-fcc9): closed seed → flip to skipped, loop back to (b). Trivial-merge (mx-fd8619): run.prUrl===null + reap.empty_push event in the persisted stream → merged directly, no GitHub poll.

content: plan-run handler step 6 (warren-f923) wires the bootPlanRunCoordinator in src/server/main.ts alongside bootScheduler with the same shutdown ordering: stop the HTTP listener first, then planRunCoordinator.stop(), then scheduler.stop(), then previewEvictionWorker.stop(), then bridges.stopAll(). This keeps any in-flight advancePlanRun call from racing with bridge/pool teardown.

PlanRun's coordinator advancePlanRun() returns a 7-shape AdvanceResult discriminated union ({kind:'dispatched'|'waiting_for_run'|'waiting_for_merge'|'advanced'|'plan_failed'|'plan_succeeded'|'noop'}) — load-bearing contract narrowed by the tick wrapper, unit tests, and the event-emit seam. SPEC §11.P documents the union as the public surface; adding or renaming a kind requires updating SPEC + tick + tests + the plan_run.* event kind set together.

PlanRun's trivial-merge advance rule: a child run that succeeded but produced zero commits (run.prUrl===null AND the reap.empty_push system event is present) advances straight to state='merged' without polling GitHub. Without this rule a docs-only or no-op plan step would deadlock the coordinator waiting for a PR that never opens. SPEC §11.P documents the rule; reap.empty_push is the canonical signal.

PlanRun's resume-on-re-dispatch contract: a second POST /plan-runs against the same planId after children have been closed out-of-band creates a fresh plan_runs row enumerating children fresh from plan.children; the coordinator probes each pending child via showSeed and flips status='closed' children to state='skipped' without spawning a run. Makes PlanRun safe to re-invoke as 'resume from the next open child' without bookkeeping about which previous plan-run owned which child. SPEC §11.P documents the contract; the dispatch handler also rejects with PlanHasNoOpenChildrenError when every child is already closed.

PlanRun's plan_run_dispatched Plot emit (warren-b89f / pl-7937 step 4) fires from POST /plan-runs in src/server/handlers.ts right after repos.planRuns.create lands the row — fire-and-log via emitPlanRunDispatchedToPlot (src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts). Gated on result.planRun.plotId !== null. Handle resolves through runs/spawn.ts's now-exported resolveDispatcherHandle so malformed user input downgrades to 'operator' instead of producing a malformed user:<handle> actor — same regex contract as single-run's run_dispatched. Payload shape is {plan_run_id, plan_id, children_count} per plot-cli schemas.ts:128. Failure surfaces as a logger.warn('plan_run.plot_append_failed') NOT an events-table row: events is keyed by run_id and no child run exists at PlanRun creation; this matches the plan_run.cancel_child_failed posture at handlers.ts:1453. Test seam: deps.planRunPlotAppender on ServerDeps; production omits it and falls through to defaultPlanRunPlotAppender which opens a UserPlotClient against join(project.localPath, '.plot') and retries once after rebuildIndex on first failure.

PlanRun coordinator Plot wiring (warren-b290 / pl-7937 step 5) threads planRun.plotId through CoordinatorSpawnFn → spawnRun in src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts (spread when plotId !== null), and adds an optional CoordinatorTransitionPlotFn hook on AdvancePlanRunInput. The hook fires once on the plan_succeeded transition when planRun.plotId !== null; coordinator maps the returned AutoTransitionResult ({kind:'transitioned'|'skipped'|'failed'}) onto plan_run.plot_auto_done / plan_run.plot_status_skipped / plan_run.plot_auto_done_failed events on the anchor child run. PLAN_RUN_EVENT_KINDS in coordinator.ts is a closed enum — adding a new kind requires extending it. The live transition impl lives in src/plan-runs/plot-transition.ts (autoTransitionPlotToDone + defaultPlotStatusSetter) and is wired in src/server/main.ts inside bootPlanRunCoordinator; it reads plot.status, guards on 'active' (drafting/ready/done/archived skip benignly), calls UserPlotClient.setStatus('done'), and logs every outcome. When transitionPlot isn't passed (tests), behavior is a no-op.

Plot UI Phase 3 release (warren-f95d, pl-9d6a step 20, v0.4.0): SPEC §11.O.Plot.UI follows the mx-49a44c five-thing template — (1) gating contract for both default-landing (someProject.hasPlot AND anyPlotExists) and per-endpoint hasPlot gates, (2) API contract table for all eight /plots* routes, (3) ACL surface narrowed via HUMANS_ONLY_EVENT_TYPES with double-validation of status transitions at handler edge, (4) ASCII data-flow diagram showing both the synchronous UI write path and the async reap→mergePlot loop converging on the ActivityFeed, (5) deferred: live event channel (pl-2047 risk #6), status-tag triggers, multi-Plot cross-references. Phase-completion release docs (SPEC subsection + README features bullet + CHANGELOG entry + two-place version pin in package.json and src/index.ts) ship in one commit per .claude/commands/release.md.

name: adopt-existing-seeds-into-plan-via-mirror-and-close
description: Workaround for sd plan submit's inability to make existing seeds children of a new plan (the very gap that warren-5561/warren-a4b7 step 4 of pl-5310 fixes upstream in seeds). Approach used for pl-5310 (warren-e40a Plot UX vision): (1) author plan JSON whose steps mirror the existing seeds' titles+descriptions verbatim, marking each with a '(warren-XXXX fold-in)' suffix per the mx-b21903 convention; (2) run sd plan submit, which spawns N new children with auto-wired step.blocks dependency graph; (3) close the ORIGINAL seeds with --reason citing the new spawn id as the canonical execution surface. Spawns become the plan's source of truth; originals remain in .seeds/issues.jsonl as historical filings closed with adoption pointers. Trade-off: mulch records that reference the originals by id keep their refs but now point at closed seeds (acceptable — closed seeds are still in jsonl, refs still resolve). Alternative (close spawns, keep originals as canonical) was rejected because it breaks plan-children semantics: closing spawns marks plan 'done' prematurely when underlying work is still in progress. Apply this pattern only when sd plan adoption is unavailable; once step 4 of pl-5310 ships the seeds-side 'existing_seeds:' field, prefer that instead.

content: SPEC §11.Q (warren-a4b7 / pl-5310 step 4 design lock, 2026-05-18) follows the cross-repo-design-lock-seed pattern (mx-bffcf1) for the Plot→synthesized plan-run pipeline: phase 1 lands §11.Q in SPEC.md + files the upstream seeds-cli dep tracker in warren's .seeds/ (warren-d519) + decomposes warren-a4b7 into a 5-step sub-plan (pl-f404: seeds-cli ship, double-pin, src/plot-plan-runs/ + POST /plot-plan-runs, PlotDetail Dispatch-as-plan-run button, scenario 30) before any code lands. The new endpoint synthesizes a throwaway parent seed + plan whose children are the Plot's open non-sd_plan seeds_issue attachments, then calls POST /plan-runs in-process with plot_id set — reusing every §11.P.Plot wiring (plan_run_dispatched event, per-child PLOT_ID injection, auto-done on final merge) unchanged. §12 plot row updated with one-line cross-ref to §11.Q.

sd plan submit's 'steps[i].blocks' field uses 0-BASED indices into the steps array — submit rejects 1-based references with 'step index N is out of range (have N steps)'. For a 5-step plan, valid indices are 0..4; semantics is 'this step blocks steps[j]'. Discovered while submitting pl-f404 (warren-a4b7 sub-plan): initial [2,3,4,5] / [3] / [4,5] / [5] failed, corrected to [1,2,3,4] / [2] / [3,4] / [4].

name: cross-repo-followup-bug-on-pinned-contract
description: When a multi-tool contract (e.g. mx-0db923 piRuntime envPassthrough widening) was pinned via the warren-0e06 step-0 coordination seed but then misimplemented downstream, the follow-up bug uses the same shape: (a) file the actual fix as a seed in the implementing repo (e.g. burrow-6f3f for warren-fe96), (b) keep the warren tracking seed open with a 'Closure gate' section enumerating the burrow seed + tagged release + warren burrow-cli triple-pin bump + acceptance scenario coverage + back-linked PRs, and (c) record cross-repo PR links back into the warren seed at closure. sd has no cross-repo dep edges, so the gate lives in seed body prose only. This avoids the temptation to close the warren tracking seed when the burrow fix lands but warren hasn't yet bumped + verified end-to-end.

content: SPEC §11.Q (POST /plot-plan-runs) phrasing 'Calls the existing POST /plan-runs handler in-process' is a CONTRACT statement, not a literal invocation. The /plot-plan-runs handler at src/server/handlers.ts createPlotPlanRunHandler inlines the post-synthesis dispatch logic (showPlan re-read + agent resolve + repos.planRuns.create + emitPlanRunDispatchedToPlot) rather than fetching its own route or calling createPlanRunHandler directly. The phrasing means same downstream effects fire: PlanRun row persists, plan_run_dispatched event lands on the Plot, per-child PLOT_ID injection works via coordinator's planRun.plotId thread-through (mx-90f430), Plot auto-transitions to done on final merge. Inlining is ~30 LOC duplication but keeps each handler's gate-stack legible.
